page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.gn6v-c8g1.2xlarge is a GPU-accelerated virtual machine instance configured with an Intel Xeon 8163 processor. This x86_64 architecture server features 8 dedicated physical cores with 8 vCPUs running at a clock speed of 2.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. A key hardware component is the single NVIDIA Volta V100 GPU, which provides 16 GB of dedicated VRAM. The instance operates on the KVM hypervisor and does not include local storage. Network connectivity is limited to a baseline bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates that the CPU and memory subsystems deliver lower-tier performance relative to modern cloud hardware. Single-core and multi-core stress-ng tests, alongside Geekbench single-core and multi-core evaluations, place the instance's processing capabilities in the bottom 10% to 25% tier. Similarly, memory bandwidth for small blocks is weak, though large block read and write operations perform at an average level. These results highlight a clear tradeoff: the instance relies on its dedicated GPU accelerator to handle intensive computations, as the host CPU and memory subsystems exhibit performance limitations.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, this instance represents a legacy GPU tier. It provides access to dedicated hardware acceleration without the premium associated with newer GPU architectures, though this is offset by lower CPU performance and the absence of local storage. The ecs.gn6v-c8g1.2xlarge is structured for workloads that can offload primary processing to the NVIDIA V100 GPU, such as legacy machine learning training, GPU-accelerated database queries, and moderate parallel computing tasks, rather than CPU-bound or high-throughput web serving applications.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.gn6v-c8g1.2xlarge is a GPU-accelerated instance featuring an Intel Xeon 8163 processor with 8 dedicated vCPUs and 8 physical cores running at 2.5 GHz. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory and a single NVIDIA Volta V100 GPU with 16 GB of VRAM. The instance does not include local storage and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. Benchmark results show that the CPU and memory performance generally fall into the bottom 10% to 25% tier, though large-block memory bandwidth remains average. This hardware profile presents a clear tradeoff, offering dedicated GPU acceleration alongside modest host processing capabilities. It is designed for workloads that offload computational tasks to the GPU, such as machine learning model training, parallel processing, and GPU-assisted data analysis, rather than general-purpose CPU-intensive operations.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.gn6v-c8g1.2xlarge is a GPU-accelerated instance featuring 8 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and an NVIDIA Volta V100 GPU with 16 GB VRAM for parallel computing workloads.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.gn6v-c8g1.2xlarge is a KVM-based instance with 8 vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and an NVIDIA V100 GPU, designed for GPU-accelerated workloads.
tagline: A GPU-accelerated virtual server pairing dedicated Intel Xeon processing with an NVIDIA Volta V100 GPU for parallel computing workloads.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated Intel Xeon 8163 vCPUs running at 2.5 GHz
1x NVIDIA Volta V100 GPU with 16 GB VRAM
32.0 GB of system memory (4.0 GB per core)
No local storage and 2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Suited for GPU-accelerated machine learning and parallel processing tasks
